question.text: A bond portfolio manager expects interest rates to rise in the next 3 months, which would cause bond prices to fall. Which interest rate option strategy would best protect the portfolio?

answer.text: Buy put options on GOI bond futures, as puts gain value when bond prices fall
Buy call options on GOI bond futures, as calls gain when rates rise
Sell put options to collect premium income as a hedge
Sell call options to offset the potential bond price decline

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Rising rates cause bond prices to fall. Put options gain value when the underlying falls, providing protection. The premium paid is the cost of this insurance. **The Trap:** Buying calls would benefit from rising bond prices, not falling ones. Selling options (puts or calls) does not provide a protective hedge -- it increases risk. **Cross-Exam Context:** Hedging with puts is a practical application tested in NISM Equity Derivatives (protective puts on stock portfolios). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This is like buying insurance on your bond portfolio. The premium is the 'insurance cost' and the strike price determines your 'deductible' (acceptable level of loss).
